sections.about: This metric measures the total number of employees disclosed at financial year-end. It falls under BRSR Section A — General Disclosures, Employees; it is not an Essential or Leadership Indicator under the principle-wise section. No direction is assigned: a larger employee base can reflect scale, legacy manpower, insourcing, or lower automation, while a smaller base can reflect productivity or outsourcing.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: In FY2024-25, Tata Steel (India entities) discloses 53,599 permanent employees, marginally ahead of SAIL at 53,159, with JSW at 14,353 and Jindal Stainless at 5,898. Tata's India entities scope covers permanent employees across its India operations, making it more directly comparable with peers than the full consolidated figure. SAIL's comparable employee base reflects its large public-sector integrated footprint spanning five integrated steel plants, special steel plants and operating mines. Tata's employee base grew from 44,914 in FY2022-23 to 53,599 in FY2024-25.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata (India entities) discloses 53,599 permanent employees — the largest in the peer set, marginally ahead of SAIL at 53,159. For management interpretation, this metric is best read alongside crude steel output, tonnes per employee, contractor intensity and automation across BF-BOF, mining, downstream and shared services.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Tata Steel data covers Tata Steel entities in India (permanent employees) across all years and is not strictly standalone (Tata Steel Limited only) or full consolidated (which includes overseas operations). All peers report on a standalone basis. Comparisons reflect permanent employees at respective India operations for Tata versus standalone for peers.
